dialogos.texto_en: Right.\nOkay, Itsuki-chan. Can you explain?
What just happened?
I apologize.\nSomething was rubbing me the wrong way.
You went a bit too far.\nFutaro wouldn't make such a decision lightly.
No, I never intended to say that he was irresponsible.\nIt's just that his decision seemed too selfish,\nso I spoke out.
I can understand your feelings, Itsuki.
Nino?
I mean, Fu-kun has been rejecting\nboth of our feelings since the after-party, you know?
Well... That's true.
But in the end, most of us were dissatisfied or worried\nabout the answer Futaro-kun gave that day.
Well, I'm just glad Fu-kun finally decided\nto give an answer.
It took a while,\nbut at least we succeeded in getting him\nto face us.
We still don't know who he will choose, though.
Yeah...
Fu-kun really intended to use this trip\nto confirm his own feelings from the beginning.
Well, it's just like him to be honest\nand straightforward when admitting it himself...\nIs he really just terrible at romance?
Quite likely.
It's hard to imagine that he wouldn't\nconsider everyone's feelings\nwhile making his decision, right?
But at least, Uesugi-san\nis thinking about us in his own way, isn't he?
The worst-case scenario\nis that he decides, again, to choose no one.
I-I don't think he's gonna do that.
...Because you want to be with him.
Eh?!
No, that's not what I meant!\nI didn't mean me!
He said that when we chatted earlier today.
I don't know if he was talking about us sisters\nor someone specific, though.
It's still quite vague. But that's just like Futaro-kun.
I think he still hasn't made up his mind\nabout his feelings.
But if he's still uncertain...\nwe can't accept half-hearted decisions like that.
So that's why this is a trial?
But how do we test Futaro's resolve?
This.
...Huh?
This is...
I see.
Itsuki, are we?
...Yes.\nThis is the only way, I believe.
